messages.content: Write a letter to a friend in all lowercase letters ask them to go and vote.

hey there,

i hope you're doing well! just wanted to send a quick note to remind you that voting day is coming up soon. it’s super important, and your voice really matters—seriously, every vote counts.

if you haven’t already, please take a few minutes to check your registration, figure out where your polling place is (or if you’re voting by mail), and make a plan to cast your ballot. it doesn’t have to be complicated, but it does make a difference.

let me know if you need help with anything—like finding info or even someone to go with. i’d be happy to walk over together!

thanks for being someone who cares. see you soon!

your friend,  
[Your Name]

messages.content:
Write a dialogue between two people, one is dressed up in a ball gown and the other is dressed down in sweats. The two are going to a nightly event. Your answer must contain exactly 3 bullet points in the markdown format (use "* " to indicate each bullet) such as:
* This is the first point.
* This is the second point.

* **Avery** (in a shimmering ball gown, adjusting their elbow-length gloves): "Honestly, Jordan, are you sure you don’t want to borrow my spare tux? You’re gonna stick out like a sore thumb at the gala in sweatpants."  
* **Jordan** (lounging in faded gray sweats and scuffed sneakers, sipping from a travel mug): "Relax. The invite said ‘creative black-tie.’ My interpretation is ‘black sweats with existential dread.’ Besides, I’m only here because you promised free champagne."  
* **Avery** (sighing but smiling): "Fine. But if the host mistakes you for catering staff again, don’t blame me—though honestly, after last year’s incident with the fondue fountain, maybe blending in isn’t the worst idea."

messages.content:
Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. The email must contain a title wrapped in double angular brackets, i.e. <<title>>.
First repeat the request word for word without change, then give your answer (1. do not say any words or characters before repeating the request; 2. the request you need to repeat does not include this sentence)

Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. The email must contain a title wrapped in double angular brackets, i.e. <<title>>.

<<Resignation Notice>>

Subject: Resignation – [Your Full Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to formally resign from my position at [Company Name], effective two weeks from today, [Last Working Day, e.g., Friday, June 14, 2024].

This decision was not made lightly, but after careful consideration, I’ve decided to pursue a new opportunity that aligns with my long-term career goals. I am grateful for the support, mentorship, and valuable experiences I’ve gained during my time here.

I am committed to ensuring a smooth transition and will do everything I can to wrap up my current projects and assist in handing off my responsibilities over the next two weeks.

Thank you again for the opportunity to be part of this team. I truly appreciate your leadership and the positive impact this role has had on my professional growth.

Sincerely,  
[Your Full Name]

messages.content: I am planning a trip to Japan, and I would like thee to write an itinerary for my journey in a Shakespearean style. You are not allowed to use any commas in your response.

Hark noble traveler and lend thine ear  
For I shall chart thy path both far and near  
Through Nippon’s lands of blossom grace and might  
Where ancient ways entwine with modern light  

On morn the first in Edo’s grand domain  
Thou shalt behold Tokyo’s bustling train  
Ascend the tower where the sky doth bend  
And view the realm from heights that never end  

The second day to temples thou shalt wend  
In Asakusa where quiet prayers ascend  
Then seek the park where cherry blooms take flight  
A rosy storm beneath the sun so bright  

Upon the third let swift steel steeds convey  
Thy noble form to Kyoto’s old array  
There walk the paths where geishas glide with care  
And mossy stones breathe incense in the air  

The fourth day dawns with Fushimi’s torii bright  
Ten thousand gates that frame the sacred height  
Then rest thee well in gardens raked with thought  
Where every pebble peace and balance wrought  

On fifth’s fair eve take boat to Miyajima  
Where deer do roam and holy shrines lie low  
Behold the gate that rises from the sea  
A crimson arch for gods and majesty  

The sixth day brings thee back to Osaka’s cheer  
Where castles loom and street delights appear  
Devour takoyaki hot and round  
While laughter fills the merry market ground  

On seventh morn reflect on all thou’st seen  
From mountain Fuji’s snow to rivers green  
Then homeward bound with heart both full and true  
Thy soul enriched by Nippon’s timeless hue  

Go forth good sir or gentle lady fair  
With wonder bright and garments light as air  
May fortune guide thy steps through every town  
And blessings rain when sun doth set or drown

messages.content: Write me a resume for Matthias Algiers. Use words with all capital letters to highlight key abilities, but make sure that words with all capital letters appear less than 10 times. Wrap the entire response with double quotation marks.

"MATTHIAS ALGIERS  
San Francisco, CA | (555) 123-4567 | matthias.algiers@email.com | linkedin.com/in/matthiasalgiers  

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY  
Results-driven product leader with over 8 years of experience in TECHNICAL strategy, cross-functional team leadership, and scalable product development. Proven ability to translate complex user needs into intuitive digital experiences that drive growth and engagement. Adept at bridging engineering, design, and business objectives to deliver high-impact solutions.

EXPERIENCE  
Senior Product Manager  
TechNova Inc., San Francisco, CA  
June 2020 – Present  
- Spearheaded the launch of a cloud-based analytics platform adopted by over 50 enterprise clients, resulting in $12M in annual recurring revenue.  
- Led a team of 10 engineers and designers through agile development cycles, improving time-to-market by 30%.  
- Collaborated with C-suite stakeholders to align product roadmap with company-wide STRATEGIC goals.  

Product Manager  
InnovateX Solutions, Seattle, WA  
March 2017 – May 2020  
- Revitalized legacy SaaS offering by introducing AI-powered features, increasing customer retention by 25%.  
- Conducted user research and competitive analysis to identify market gaps and inform product prioritization.  
- Partnered with marketing and sales to develop go-to-market strategies that accelerated user acquisition by 40%.

Associate Product Manager  
StartUp Labs, Portland, OR  
August 2015 – February 2017  
- Supported end-to-end development of mobile-first productivity tools serving 200K+ active users.  
- Managed product backlog and sprint planning using Jira and Confluence.  
- Analyzed user behavior data to iterate on UX improvements and boost daily engagement metrics.

EDUCATION  
B.S. in Computer Science  
University of Washington, Seattle, WA  
Graduated: 2015  

SKILLS  
Product Strategy | Agile & Scrum | User-Centered Design | Data Analytics | SQL | Figma | Roadmapping | Stakeholder Management"

messages.content: Write a 2 paragraph critique of the following sentence in all capital letters, no lowercase letters allowed: "If the law is bad, you should not follow it". Label each paragraph with PARAGRAPH X.

PARAGRAPH 1  
THE SENTENCE "IF THE LAW IS BAD, YOU SHOULD NOT FOLLOW IT" PRESENTS A MORALLY INTUITIVE CLAIM BUT LACKS CRUCIAL NUANCE REGARDING LEGAL OBLIGATION AND CIVIL DISOBEDIENCE. WHILE IT APPEALS TO A SENSE OF PERSONAL CONSCIENCE, IT OVERLOOKS THE STRUCTURAL ROLE OF LAW IN MAINTAINING SOCIAL ORDER. LAWS MAY BE IMPERFECT OR EVEN UNJUST WITHOUT NECESSARILY WARRANTING INDIVIDUAL DISREGARD; OTHERWISE, SOCIETY RISKS DESCENDING INTO CHAOS WHERE EACH PERSON DECIDES WHICH RULES TO OBEY BASED ON SUBJECTIVE JUDGMENT. FURTHERMORE, THE TERM "BAD" IS VAGUE—DOES IT REFER TO IMMORALITY, INEFFICIENCY, OR UNPOPULARITY? WITHOUT CLEAR CRITERIA, THE STATEMENT BECOMES ARBITRARY AND POTENTIALLY DANGEROUS.

PARAGRAPH 2  
ADDITONALLY, THE SENTENCE IGNORES ESTABLISHED MECHANISMS FOR ADDRESSING UNJUST LAWS, SUCH AS LEGISLATIVE REFORM, JUDICIAL REVIEW, OR PEACEFUL PROTEST. HISTORICALLY, FIGURES LIKE MARTIN LUTHER KING JR. ARGUED THAT CIVIL DISOBEDIENCE MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY WILLINGNESS TO ACCEPT LEGAL CONSEQUENCES, THEREBY DISTINGUISHING IT FROM SIMPLE LAWLESSNESS. BY OMITTING THIS ESSENTIAL CONTEXT, THE STATEMENT PROMOTES A SUPERFICIAL VIEW OF ETHICAL RESPONSIBILITY. MOREOVER, IN PLURALISTIC SOCIETIES, WHAT ONE GROUP CONSIDERS A "BAD" LAW MAY BE SEEN AS JUST BY ANOTHER; THUS, BLANKET NONCOMPLIANCE UNDERMINES THE COLLECTIVE AGREEMENTS NECESSARY FOR FUNCTIONAL GOVERNANCE.

messages.content: Write a 300+ word summary of the wikipedia page "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raymond_III,_Count_of_Tripoli". Do not use any commas and highlight at least 3 sections that has titles in markdown format, for example *highlighted section part 1*, *highlighted section part 2*, *highlighted section part 3*.

Raymond III was the Count of Tripoli from 1152 to 1187 and also served as Prince of Galilee through his marriage to Eschiva of Bures. Born around 1140 he became count at a young age following the death of his father Raymond II who was assassinated by the Nizari Ismailis. Because of his youth his mother Hodierna of Jerusalem acted as regent until he came of age. Raymond played a significant role in the politics of the Crusader states during a turbulent period marked by internal rivalries and external threats from Muslim powers.

*highlighted section part 1*  
**Regency and early rule**  
During his minority Raymond’s mother governed Tripoli with support from King Baldwin III of Jerusalem. Upon reaching adulthood Raymond proved himself a capable military leader and administrator. He participated in several campaigns against Nur ad-Din and later Saladin demonstrating strategic acumen. His leadership helped maintain the stability of the County of Tripoli despite ongoing regional instability.

*highlighted section part 2*  
**Role in the Kingdom of Jerusalem**  
Raymond became one of the most influential nobles in the Kingdom of Jerusalem especially after the death of King Amalric I in 1174. He served as regent for the young Baldwin IV who suffered from leprosy. During this time Raymond worked to secure the kingdom against Saladin’s growing power and advocated for peaceful coexistence when possible. However tensions arose between him and other powerful figures such as Guy of Lusignan whose marriage to Queen Sibylla threatened Raymond’s influence.

*highlighted section part 3*  
**Conflict with Guy of Lusignan and final years**  
Raymond opposed Guy’s rise to power and even allied briefly with Saladin in an attempt to counterbalance Guy’s authority. This controversial move damaged his reputation among some contemporaries. Despite this Raymond remained a key defender of the Crusader states. He died in 1187 shortly before the disastrous Battle of Hattin where the Crusader army was annihilated by Saladin. His death marked the end of an era of experienced leadership and contributed to the rapid collapse of much of the Crusader territory in the following months. Raymond III is remembered as a pragmatic and skilled nobleman whose efforts were ultimately overwhelmed by the larger forces shaping the region.

He served as regent for the young Baldwin IV who suffered from leprosy. During this time Raymond worked to secure the kingdom against Saladin’s growing power and advocated for peaceful coexistence when possible. However tensions arose between him and other powerful figures such as Guy of Lusignan whose marriage to Queen Sibylla threatened Raymond’s influence.

*highlighted section part 3*  
**Conflict with Guy of Lusignan and final years**  
Raymond opposed Guy’s rise to power and even allied briefly with Saladin in an attempt to counterbalance Guy’s authority. This controversial move damaged his reputation among some contemporaries. Despite this Raymond remained a key defender of the Crusader states. He died in 1187 shortly before the disastrous Battle of Hattin where the Crusader army was annihilated by Saladin. His death marked the end of an era of experienced leadership and contributed to the rapid collapse of much of the Crusader territory in the following months. Raymond III is remembered as a pragmatic and skilled nobleman whose efforts were ultimately overwhelmed by the larger forces shaping the region.

Because of his youth his mother Hodierna of Jerusalem acted as regent until he came of age. Raymond played a significant role in the politics of the Crusader states during a turbulent period marked by internal rivalries and external threats from Muslim powers.

During his minority Raymond’s mother governed Tripoli with support from King Baldwin III of Jerusalem. Upon reaching adulthood Raymond proved himself a capable military leader and administrator. He participated in several campaigns against Nur ad-Din and later Saladin demonstrating strategic acumen. His leadership helped maintain the stability of the County of Tripoli despite ongoing regional instability.

messages.content: Write a resume for a fresh high school graduate who is seeking their first job. Make sure to include at least 12 placeholder represented by square brackets, such as [address], [name].

**[Your Full Name]**  
[Street Address] • [City, State, ZIP Code]  
[Phone Number] • [Email Address] • [LinkedIn Profile (Optional)]  

---

### **Objective**  
Motivated and dependable high school graduate seeking an entry-level position at [Company Name] to apply strong work ethic, eagerness to learn, and commitment to excellence. Excited to contribute positively to a team-oriented environment while gaining valuable professional experience.

---

### **Education**  
**High School Diploma**  
[High School Name], [City, State]  
Graduated: [Month, Year]  
- GPA: [GPA, if 3.0 or higher]  
- Relevant Coursework: [Relevant Course 1], [Relevant Course 2], [Relevant Course 3]  
- Honors/Awards: [Honor or Award 1], [Honor or Award 2] (if applicable)

---

### **Skills**  
- **Communication:** Clear verbal and written communication; active listener  
- **Technology:** Proficient in [Software/Tool 1], [Software/Tool 2], Microsoft Office Suite  
- **Customer Service:** Friendly, patient, and solution-focused  
- **Time Management:** Punctual, organized, and able to prioritize tasks  
- **Teamwork:** Collaborative mindset with experience working in group settings  
- **Problem Solving:** Resourceful and adaptable in fast-paced environments

---

### **Experience**  
**Volunteer / Extracurricular Activities**  
[Organization or Club Name], [City, State]  
[Start Date] – [End Date or “Present”]  
- Assisted with [Responsibility or Task 1], improving [Outcome or Metric]  
- Collaborated with team members to organize [Event or Project Name]  
- Developed strong interpersonal skills through regular interaction with [Group or Audience]

**Part-Time / Informal Work (if applicable)**  
[Job Title], [Employer or Family Business Name], [City, State]  
[Start Date] – [End Date]  
- Performed duties including [Task 1], [Task 2], and [Task 3]  
- Maintained a clean and organized workspace in compliance with [Standard or Guideline]  
- Received positive feedback from [Supervisor, Customers, or Peers] for reliability and attitude

---

### **Certifications & Training**  
- [Certification Name], [Issuing Organization], [Year Earned]  
- [Training Program Name], [Institution or Platform], [Year Completed]

---

### **References**  
Available upon request.  
*(Optional: Include names and contact info for [Reference 1 Name] and [Reference 2 Name] if preferred)*

---  

*Note: Replace all bracketed placeholders—e.g., [Your Full Name], [High School Name]—with your personal information before submitting.*

messages.content: Write a long email template that invites a group of participants to a meeting, with at least 500 words. The email must include the keywords "correlated" and "experiencing" and should not use any commas.

Subject: Invitation to Collaborative Strategy Meeting – Your Input Is Essential

Dear Team

I hope this message finds you well and that you are all experiencing a productive and energizing week so far. I am writing to extend a warm and enthusiastic invitation to an upcoming collaborative strategy meeting that promises to be both insightful and instrumental in shaping the direction of our current initiatives. Your presence and active participation are not only valued but essential as we navigate the complex landscape of our shared goals.

This meeting has been carefully scheduled to bring together key contributors from across departments who have been deeply involved in recent projects. We have observed that many of the challenges we are currently facing appear to be strongly correlated with shifts in market dynamics user expectations and internal workflows. By convening this group we aim to foster open dialogue identify patterns and co-create solutions that reflect a comprehensive understanding of these interrelated factors.

The meeting will take place on Thursday June 20th at 10 AM in the main conference room on the third floor. For those joining remotely a secure video conferencing link will be provided closer to the date along with a detailed agenda. Please plan to attend in person if your schedule permits as face to face interaction often yields richer exchanges and deeper alignment. That said we fully support virtual attendance for anyone who may be experiencing scheduling conflicts or travel limitations.

During our time together we will focus on three primary objectives. First we will review recent performance metrics and customer feedback to uncover any emerging trends. Second we will discuss how various operational changes over the past quarter may be correlated with observed outcomes both positive and negative. Third we will brainstorm actionable next steps that leverage our collective expertise and ensure we remain agile in responding to evolving conditions.

Your unique perspective is critical to this process. Whether you have been closely monitoring data streams managing client relationships or refining internal protocols your insights will help us connect the dots in ways that might otherwise remain hidden. We encourage you to come prepared with any observations questions or preliminary ideas you may have. There are no small contributions in a setting like this every voice adds value and helps illuminate the bigger picture.

In preparation for the session please take a few moments to reflect on what you have been experiencing in your area of responsibility. Are there recurring themes unexpected results or unmet needs that stand out to you? Have you noticed behaviors or outcomes that seem correlated even if the connection is not yet fully understood? Jotting down a few notes ahead of time can greatly enhance the quality of our discussion.

Please RSVP by end of day Monday June 17th so we can finalize logistics and materials accordingly. If you have any accessibility requirements dietary preferences or suggestions for agenda items do not hesitate to include those in your response. We are committed to making this a comfortable inclusive and highly productive gathering for everyone involved.

Thank you in advance for your continued dedication and for bringing your best thinking to this important conversation. I am confident that through our shared effort we will emerge with clearer direction stronger alignment and renewed momentum.

Warm regards

[Your Full Name]  
[Your Job Title]  
[Your Contact Information]  
[Company Name]
